mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in industrial production signals expanding manufacturing output and rising corporate earnings, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day S&P 500 returns occur in fewer than 55% of months when INDPRO increases month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5% reflects restrictive monetary policy that elevates discount rates and borrowing costs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Joint growth in retail sales alongside a falling unemployment rate signals robust consumer demand and labor market health, which together amplify corporate revenue expectations and drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between RSAFS growth and UNRATE declines produces a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In the aftermath of a macroeconomic shock, a sustained increase in housing starts signals stabilizing credit conditions and recovering residential investment, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST increases during post-shock recovery windows coincide with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in nonfarm payrolls signal accelerating labor market contraction and collapsing aggregate demand, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines month-over-month for two consecutive months
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances triggered by two consecutive monthly payroll declines.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated nominal interest rates combined with decelerating consumer price inflation indicate rising real interest rates, which jointly tighten financial conditions and depress equity valuations over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of high FEDFUNDS and low CPIAUCSL momentum fails to produce negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of joint occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding a 4% annualized threshold erodes real corporate profit margins and increases the probability of Federal Reserve tightening, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 60% of months when CPIAUCSL year-over-year change exceeds 4%.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP confirms broad-based economic expansion and supports aggregate demand expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 surprises are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of quarterly observations.
